Defines functions and types for managing Out-Of-Band (OOB) Bluetooth pairing in Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This C header file provides an interface for managing Out-Of-Band (OOB) data exchange during Bluetooth pairing procedures. It defines a function pointer type, `BLESecurityOOBHandler`, which applications can implement to supply OOB keys for specific devices. The function [`ble_security_set_oob_handler`](<#ble_security_set_oob_handler>) allows registration of a callback function that provides OOB keys when required during pairing. Additionally, the function [`ble_security_enable_oob`](<#ble_security_enable_oob>) enables or disables OOB pairing for a specified device, indicating that the application can provide OOB data for secure pairing. The OOB mechanism enhances security by using a separate communication channel to exchange pairing keys, reducing the risk of eavesdropping.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `bluetooth/bluetooth_types.h`
